title: Epistemic Stack — Claim Graph
description: A provenanced, typed claim graph over a multi-source dispute. Designed to be interoperable and compounding: another team can extend this artifact, and pieces can be merged across investigations.

model.description: Model + version used for extraction, for reproducibility.

sources.description: Every source ingested. Provenance starts here.

side.description: Which position the source argues for, if any — used to detect 'performed settling' and to measure cross-side edges. Values are case-specific (e.g. covid: zoonosis | lab_leak; black holes / eggs: no_risk | risk | depends; plus neutral | n/a), so this is a free string rather than a fixed enum.

claims.description: Atomic claims. Each is a node in the graph.

text.description: The claim, normalised to one proposition.

attestations.description: Every place this claim (or a restatement) appears. Multiple attestations = the same claim seen across sources.

verbatim_span.description: Exact quoted text from the source. Non-negotiable: every claim must be auditable back to source.

context.description: Surrounding context / where in the source.

framing.description: How THIS source framed it, when it differs from the canonical text (captures 'similar-but-not-identical').

probability_estimates.description: Where analysts attached an explicit probability/likelihood to this claim. Makes the multi-analyst estimate spread navigable.

edges.description: Typed relationships between claims. This is the inference + discourse structure.

type.description: Relationship. 'depends_on' marks load-bearing structure for crux detection. 'context_mutation' marks where two sides accept the same facts but build competing causal narratives around them — a frame disagreement, NOT an evidential contradiction, and deliberately excluded from concentration weight.

source_id.description: Which source asserts this relationship (a relationship is itself a claim).

strength.description: How strongly the relationship is held / how contested it is.

assessment.description: Output of the assessment layer. Kept separate from structure so consumers with different beliefs can re-run it.

cruxes.description: Ranked load-bearing claims: flipping these most changes a conclusion.

sensitivity.description: 0-1, how much the conclusion moves if this flips.

correlated_evidence_flags.description: Sets of claims treated as independent that may share a common source/cause.
